frameworks.name: amortized inference
frameworks.one_line: Reuse stored summaries of past inferences to approximate new ones.

frameworks.name: Bayesian models of cognition
frameworks.one_line: Probabilistic reasoning extended with cost-aware computational reuse.

frameworks.name: resource-rational analysis
frameworks.one_line: Brain trades accuracy against cognitive cost under limited resources.

core_question: Do people reuse computations from prior probabilistic inferences when answering related queries?
key_assumptions: People perform probabilistic inference over hypotheses
Full Bayesian computations are intractable or expensive
Closely related queries can share computational substrate
manipulations: Relatedness between sequential queries about natural scenes
Structure / content of the first query
Cognitive load during the inference task
key_findings: Second-query responses systematically depend on first-query structure
This carryover appears only when the two queries are related
Under cognitive load, people reuse summary statistics rather than full distributions
interpretations: Humans amortize inference by caching summaries of past computations
Probabilistic reasoning trades accuracy against computational cost
